questionText: Choose the correct treasure-box code. The code is a 2-digit number. It is greater than 40 and less than 80. It is even. Its digits add to 10. When the digits are swapped, the new number is 27 more than the original code.
subject: Maths
learningObjective: Students can identify relationships and apply deductive reasoning
issues: [concept_accuracy, explanation_quality, clarity] | The item is mathematically inconsistent: no option satisfies all clues, especially the condition that the swapped number is 27 more than the original. | The marked correct answer (64) is incorrect; swapping 64 gives 46, which is 18 less, not 27 more. | The explanation explicitly shows that none of the options fit, then still selects 64, so the explanation and answer key are self-contradictory. | Fix: Revise the numerical clue so exactly one option works, or replace the options with a valid set containing the true solution. | Fix: Update the answer key to match the corrected clues. | Fix: Rewrite the explanation to show a consistent valid solution path without contradiction.
